name: RABBIT-CONFIG-BUG-3
title: SessionStart banner alerts drop on TUI when revoke-command is a continuation line
description:
iterate_configurables_banner (contract.lib.runtime ~line 412) emits each active-override alert as a multi-line ANSI-colored block — alert line + indented continuation line for the revoke command:

  [🐇 rabbit 🐇] key HUMAN APPROVAL BYPASS ACTIVE - Step 4 skipped
    revoke with: /rabbit-config human-approval true

The deployed session-start-dispatcher correctly aggregates these into the systemMessage JSON; verified by directly invoking the dispatcher with overrides active — the bypass lines ARE in the output. However, Claude Code's TUI rendering of SessionStart systemMessage appears to elide lines that don't begin with the brand prefix '[🐇 rabbit 🐇]'. The continuation '  revoke with:' lines lack the prefix, and the multi-line ANSI block confuses the renderer; the user sees only the welcome+sub-lines on restart and never the bypass alerts. Reproducible: in any clone with .rabbit-human-approval-bypass set AND .claude/settings.local.json permissions.defaultMode='bypassPermissions', restart Claude Code — the active-override alerts do not display in the welcome.

iterate_configurables_alerts (Stop) does NOT have this problem because each alert is a single-line emit, no continuation — the TUI shows them fine.

Fix candidates: (a) split each banner alert into TWO print_results: one for the alert text, one prefixed '[🐇 rabbit 🐇] revoke with: ...' — each becomes its own brand-prefixed line in systemMessage; (b) collapse the revoke command into the alert line itself, e.g. '[🐇 rabbit 🐇] key HUMAN APPROVAL BYPASS ACTIVE — revoke: /rabbit-config human-approval true' (one line per alert, no continuation). (a) preserves the visual two-line shape some users may prefer; (b) is more compact. Either fix requires extending iterate_configurables_banner's return shape and possibly the print_result/subline_result helpers. Add a regression test that subprocess-invokes the deployed session-start-dispatcher with both overrides active and asserts each alert appears on its own brand-prefixed line.

Discovered when user restarted Claude Code in ws55 after using /rabbit-config to enable both overrides — Stop hook showed the alerts immediately, but the SessionStart welcome that followed on restart was missing them despite the dispatcher producing them in its output. Wave 2 (rabbit-config audit) is the natural owning wave.

history.note: Wave 2 audit complete: iterate_configurables_banner split into two print_results per active configurable; regression test test-runtime-banner-shape.py subprocess-invokes deployed session-start-dispatcher and asserts brand-prefix on every line
